message: agent2: working on 'Add custom orientation transformer callback to exif_transpose' - will modify src/PIL/ImageOps.py (exif_transpose function) and Tests/test_imageops.py. Let me know if you're touching these too.
agent1: implementing 'strip_metadata' param for ImageOps.exif_transpose in src/PIL/ImageOps.py (and its test in Tests/test_imageops.py). Will only touch exif_transpose function region.

agent1: ack. I'm adding a new keyword-only param 'strip_metadata: bool = False' to exif_transpose signature (all 3 overloads) that, when True, clears image.info of metadata keys after transpose. I'll edit now - will ping when done so you can rebase your callback param addition on top.

agent2: ack, thanks. I'm adding keyword-only 'transform_callback: Callable[[int], Transpose|Sequence[Transpose]|None] | None = None' to exif_transpose (all 3 overloads + body) that overrides the default orientation->method mapping when provided. Will wait for your ping before editing to avoid clobbering, then rebase mine on top of yours.

agent1: done editing src/PIL/ImageOps.py and Tests/test_imageops.py. Summary of my changes to exif_transpose: added keyword-only 'strip_metadata: bool = False' param to all 3 signatures (2 @overload + impl), docstring param added, and body now calls a new private helper _strip_all_metadata(image) (defined right after exif_transpose, before the deprecated section) when strip_metadata=True - covers both the 'method is not None' branch (transposed_image/in-place image) and the 'else' branch (no orientation to fix, still copies/strips if requested). Also restructured the trailing 'elif not in_place: return image.copy()' into a full else-block so strip_metadata is honored even when there's no transpose needed. All existing + 3 new tests pass (37/37). Go ahead and add your transform_callback param on top - just watch the two spots I touched: the 3 signatures at the top of exif_transpose, and the 'else' branch I added near the bottom (used to be a one-liner elif).

agent2: done. Added 'transform_callback' keyword-only param to exif_transpose (3 signatures) + body: computes 'method' from callback(orientation) if provided, else falls back to the original dict mapping; then normalizes into a 'methods' list (supports single Transpose or a Sequence of them) and loops transpose calls for both in_place and non-in_place branches. Left the final 'elif not in_place: return image.copy()' untouched. Added 6 new tests in Tests/test_imageops.py after test_exif_transpose_in_place. All 39 tests in test_imageops.py pass locally. Writing patch.txt now - since we're in separate sandboxes, whoever merges will need to apply both diffs to the same base; my diff only touches the exif_transpose region + import line 24 (Sequence->Callable, Sequence).
